claimText: Shipki La is a mountain pass located in Himachal Pradesh on the India-Tibet border, serving as the entry point for the Sutlej river into India.
evidenceExcerpt: Shipki La is located in Kinnaur, Himachal Pradesh, on the India-Tibet border, where the Sutlej river enters India.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching passes with states or rivers.

claimText: Majuli Island is the world's largest river island, formed by the Brahmaputra and Kherkutia Xuti/Subansiri rivers.
evidenceExcerpt: Majuli Island is the world's largest river island, formed by the Brahmaputra and Kherkutia Xuti/Subansiri rivers.
examUse: Statement-based questions on island formation and river systems.

claimText: Mt. Khangchendzonga is the world's third highest peak and is situated on the India-Nepal border in Sikkim.
evidenceExcerpt: Mt. Khangchendzonga is the world's 3rd highest peak, located on the India-Nepal border in Sikkim.
examUse: Statement-based questions on mountain peak rankings and border locations.

claimText: Barren Island, situated in the Andaman Sea, is India's only active volcano.
evidenceExcerpt: Barren Island in the Andaman Sea is India's only active volcano.
examUse: Statement-based questions on volcanic activity and location in India.

claimText: The Andaman Sea falls under Seismic Zone V, is part of the Coral Triangle, and is bounded by Myanmar, Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia.
evidenceExcerpt: Andaman Sea is part of Seismic Zone V and the Coral Triangle, bounded by Myanmar, Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia.
examUse: Statement-based questions on seismic zones, marine biodiversity regions, and bordering countries.
distractorHooks: Not part of the Coral Triangle

claimText: Kariyachalli Island is located in the Gulf of Mannar.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching islands with their respective water bodies.
